perturbation.original_text: 1.1 "SOFTWARE USER" means a named user of the Corio Services worldwide to whom a user identification number and password has been assigned, which permits that user to access and use the Software on a designated Corio Server.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 "SOFTWARE USER" means any individual who has access to the Corio Services to whom a user identification number and password has been assigned, which permits that user to access and use the Software on any server, designated or not.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of “SOFTWARE USER” restricts access to a “designated Corio Server”. By changing the definition to 'any server, designated or not', there is an internal contradiction with section 2.1, which specifies installation on “Corio Servers”. Thus, the users may now access the software on any server, not just those belonging to Corio.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 HOSTING SOFTWARE LICENSE. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Changepoint hereby grants to Corio a fee-bearing, irrevocable, nonexclusive, nontransferable (except in accordance with Section 14.1 of this Agreement) license in the Territory to (i) reproduce the Software in machine executable object code format only for installation on the Corio Servers; (ii) install multiple copies of the Software on Corio's Servers which will be made remotely accessible to Corio's Customers for their internal business purposes, (iii) permit limited access to and use of the Software by Customers through Corio Servers solely for such Customer's internal business purposes; (iv) sublicense an unlimited number of Customers to access and use the Software only through the installation on Corio Servers solely for such Customer's internal business purposes; and (v) use Changepoint's tools and utilities, if any, subject to any restrictions placed on the Changepoint by third party software providers and payment of any applicable fees required by such third parties, to configure, integrate and manage the Software.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts software user access to a designated Corio Server, while the changed text allows access on any server. This directly contradicts section 2.1, which licenses the software for installation specifically on Corio Servers. The change removes the requirement for the server to be a Corio server, fundamentally altering the scope of the license and creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.6 "CUSTOMER(S)" means one or more customers of the Corio Services having its principal executive offices in North America who obtains a sublicense from Corio to use the Software by accessing one or more Corio Servers.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 "CUSTOMER(S)" means any entity that utilizes Corio Services who obtains a sublicense from Corio to use the Software, irrespective of their location.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition limits the 'CUSTOMERS' to those 'having its principal executive offices in North America'. By removing this restriction, it contradicts section 2.1 which grants Corio the rights to license and host software within the territory which is defined in 1.11 as 'throughout the world'. By altering the definition to remove the location it will be a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 HOSTING SOFTWARE LICENSE. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Changepoint hereby grants to Corio a fee-bearing, irrevocable, nonexclusive, nontransferable (except in accordance with Section 14.1 of this Agreement) license in the Territory to (i) reproduce the Software in machine executable object code format only for installation on the Corio Servers; (ii) install multiple copies of the Software on Corio's Servers which will be made remotely accessible to Corio's Customers for their internal business purposes, (iii) permit limited access to and use of the Software by Customers through Corio Servers solely for such Customer's internal business purposes; (iv) sublicense an unlimited number of Customers to access and use the Software only through the installation on Corio Servers solely for such Customer's internal business purposes; and (v) use Changepoint's tools and utilities, if any, subject to any restrictions placed on the Changepoint by third party software providers and payment of any applicable fees required by such third parties, to configure, integrate and manage the Software. Corio shall not authorize Customers to download or reproduce the Software for use except as necessary in connection with the Corio Services. Except as specifically authorized by this Agreement, no license is granted under this Agreement to Corio to distribute the Software to its Customers or for use other than as part of the Corio Services.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts 'CUSTOMER(S)' to those with principal executive offices in North America. The changed text broadens the definition to any entity utilizing Corio Services, regardless of location. This directly contradicts section 2.1, which grants Corio a license 'in the Territory' (defined elsewhere), implying a geographical limitation to the license. By removing the North America restriction, the changed definition allows Corio to sublicense customers outside of the intended 'Territory,' conflicting with the geographical limitations imposed in the Hosting Software License granted in section 2.1. The removal creates a direct conflict making the modified definition incompatible with the license grant.

perturbation.original_text: 1.10 "SOFTWARE" means Changepoint's proprietary software described in EXHIBIT A attached hereto and made a part hereof, in object code form only, and any Updates or Upgrades (as defined below) thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.10 "SOFTWARE" means any software that can perform similar functions as Changepoint's proprietary software and may include software developed by Changepoint or third-party vendors and includes any Updates or Upgrades (as defined below) thereto.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'SOFTWARE' is explicitly linked to Changepoint's proprietary software, whereas the modified version broadens it to include 'any software that can perform similar functions' regardless of ownership. This change directly contradicts Section 7.2, the Intellectual Property Warranty, which states that Changepoint warrants it is the sole and exclusive owner of the Software, thus creating a legal and operational contradiction regarding who owns the rights to the 'SOFTWARE' being licensed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.2 INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY WARRANTY. Changepoint represents and warrants to Corio that (a) Changepoint is the sole and exclusive owner of the Software or is a licensee of the Software; (b) Changepoint has full and sufficient right, title and authority to grant the rights and/or licenses granted to Corio under this Agreement; (c) the Software does not contain any materials developed by a third party used by Changepoint except pursuant to a license agreement; and (d) the Software does not, to the best of Changepoint's knowledge and belief, infringe any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark or other intellectual property rights of a third party.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'SOFTWARE' broadens its scope to include any software performing similar functions, regardless of ownership, which directly contradicts Section 7.2 stating Changepoint is the 'sole and exclusive owner of the Software'. This creates a contradiction because the warranty only applies to software owned by Changepoint, but the definition now encompasses software potentially owned by third parties.
